description: Mammals produce 'milura', a nutritive liquid that feeds their young. The word also covers that liquid when gathered or prepared as food and drink. Its source and treatment matter whenever suitability is in question.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced nasal 'm' and release into high-front 'i'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l', move into stressed high-back rounded 'u', then tap 'r' and finish on open 'a'.
usage_notes: A plant-based drink may place its source before 'milura' when speakers deliberately use the milk analogy. An exact product name or ingredient statement uses its original wording when identity or safety depends on it.

semantic_domains.nature: a nutritive liquid produced by mammals for their young
semantic_domains.physical: milk handled as food, drink, or ingredient
